title: Shepherd's pie
subtitle: Lamb mince with vegetables and gravy under creamy mash
excerpt: Minced lamb cooked with onion, carrots, and stock into a rich gravy, topped with mashed potato and baked until golden. Shepherd's pie uses lamb; cottage pie uses beef. Both follow the same method. A weeknight and Sunday staple across British households.
sourceNotes: The distinction between shepherd's pie (lamb) and cottage pie (beef) is consistent in British English. The shepherd's pie name connects to the sheep-farming tradition — shepherds would have access to lamb. The dish appears in this form from the late 19th century, when mechanical mincing made minced meat widely available.

recipe.freezeNotes: Assemble and freeze before baking. Defrost fully, then bake as below adding 10 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Make two at once. The mince base scales directly.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the mince filling up to 2 days ahead, refrigerate, top with mash, and bake when needed.

content.text: The filling should be saucy but not watery. Cook it until the liquid has reduced to a thick gravy that coats the back of a spoon. If it is too liquid when you add the mash, the mash will sink and the base will be watery.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add the {{onion}} and {{carrot}} and cook for 8 minutes until softened. Increase the heat to high, add the {{lamb-mince}}, and cook, breaking it up, until browned — about 8 minutes. Add the {{tomato-puree}} and {{rosemary}}. Stir for 2 minutes. Add the {{worcestershire-sauce}} and {{stock-beef}}. Season with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Simmer uncovered for 20 minutes until the sauce is thick. Spoon into a baking dish.

Heat the oven to 190°C. Boil the {{potato}} in salted water until completely tender. Drain and steam-dry. Mash with the {{unsalted-butter}} and warm {{whole-milk}} until smooth. Season well. Spoon over the filling and spread to the edges, sealing in the mince. Rough up the surface with a fork. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until the top is golden and the filling is bubbling at the edges.

items.symptom: Mash slides off the filling when portioning
items.cause: Filling too wet or too hot when the mash was added
items.fix: Reduce the filling until it holds its shape. Allow it to cool for 5 minutes before adding the mash

items.symptom: Greasy pool at the bottom after baking
items.cause: Lamb mince too fatty, or fat not drained after browning
items.fix: Drain off excess fat after browning the mince before adding the other ingredients. Lamb shoulder mince has more fat than leg mince

content.text: Shepherd's pie is eaten on weeknights and Sundays throughout Britain. It is one of the most reliably popular dishes across generations and is rarely refused at a table with children. The filling freezes well and the assembled pie is better made the day before, giving the mash time to firm up against the filling.
